prose:
Fukui functions (electrophilic f+, nucleophilic f−, and dual descriptor) were computed for ferrocene with the chemkit `fukui-reactivity` skill at the GFN2-xTB level (xtb backend), gas phase.  
• Geometry: taken as-is from /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/ferrocene/ferrocene.xyz (no re-optimization).  
• Electronic states: neutral (charge 0, multiplicity 1), cation (charge +1, multiplicity 2), and anion (charge –1, multiplicity 2) were each run as single-point GFN2-xTB calculations; Fukui indices were obtained by finite-difference of Mulliken charges.  
• Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).  
• Software: xtb 6.x (called via chemkit), tight-binding Hamiltonian GFN2-xTB. SCF cycles converged automatically (xtb default).  

Headline result – most electrophilic site: Fe atom (index 11, 1-based) with f+ = 0.22885456 (dimensionless). The same Fe atom is also the most nucleophilic with f− = 0.25938191.  

Live log: the run streamed to the file fukui_<timestamp>.out in the working directory; it can be inspected or tailed for SCF details and convergence history.  

Warnings from chemkit (verbatim):  
• sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)  
• sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)  
• common header present  

The integrity check reports status “ok” with trustworthy = true. All required conservation tests (Σf≈1) passed.  

The values reported are computed; no experimental reference is supplied here.
